geographic_scope: Mixed; empire-wide deportation corridors and killing sites
admin_units: deportation routes toward the Syrian desert
theater_description: Subnational but moving, corridor-style theater spanning arrest sites, deportation routes, and desert concentration/annihilation zones.
geometry_method_note: Would require route-based polygons rather than a single national denominator.
military_area_note: Military victim exposure denominator is unresolved and likely negligible relative to civilian-targeted killing.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure changed rapidly during deportation and concentration.
split_reason: Geography is mobile and cannot be represented by a whole-country denominator.
quality_note: Screened only. Victim counts are better established than any episode-specific military exposure denominator; DR would likely be censored or non-estimable.

episode_name: Nanjing core urban massacre and adjacent execution zones
geographic_scope: City-level plus adjacent peri-urban execution sites
theater_description: City fall, safety-zone terror, and nearby mass execution sites after the Japanese capture of Nanjing.
geometry_method_note: Should be split at minimum into city-core civilian terror and separate POW/execution-site subepisodes.
military_area_note: POW and routed-soldier exposures may be estimable, but not yet harmonized to the civilian terror theater.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure depends on city population collapse and Safety Zone concentration.
split_reason: Civilian massacre and military/POW execution processes likely need separate denominators.
quality_note: Screened only. Promising for subepisode work, but current bundle does not yet defend a joint civilian/military exposure denominator.

episode_name: Warsaw Ghetto Uprising and final liquidation
theater_description: The sealed Warsaw Ghetto during the April-May 1943 uprising and liquidation campaign.
geometry_method_note: A city-block polygon is feasible; this is one of the more promising genocide episodes for denominator work.
military_area_note: Jewish fighter strength is roughly bounded, but fighter death totals are not cleanly separated from broader victim totals in the sources reviewed here.
civilian_area_note: Competing source estimates place the remaining ghetto population around 55,000-60,000 or 70,000-80,000 before the final action.
split_reason: Compact urban theater with a bounded insurgent force, but still requires cleaner status separation.
quality_note: Screened only. Best candidate for follow-on work, but publication is withheld until fighter-vs-civilian death separation is better defended.

theater_description: Repeated rural-clearance offensives, village destruction, detention, and mass killing across Kurdish northern Iraq.
geometry_method_note: Would require phase-by-phase governorate or valley-level polygons.
military_area_note: Peshmerga exposure exists but is not yet matched to the civilian-clearing subepisodes in a way suitable for DR.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure is strongly affected by village destruction, detention, and internal displacement.
split_reason: Campaign is too heterogeneous for a single denominator and should be split by offensive phase.
quality_note: Screened only. Strong atrocity documentation, weaker phase-specific military exposure denominators.

geographic_scope: National but highly uneven subnational intensity
theater_description: Nationwide exterminatory violence with sharp prefectural variation and contemporaneous civil-war combat.
geometry_method_note: Needs prefecture-level episodeization rather than a single country-wide denominator.
military_area_note: Civilian victim counts are much stronger than any genocide-theater military person-month series aligned to the same places and weeks.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure varied sharply by prefecture, roadblock density, and RPF advance.
split_reason: Whole-country denominators would be misleading; prefectural subepisodes are necessary.
quality_note: Screened only. Potentially estimable in prefecture-level slices, but not defended here.

episode_name: Srebrenica fall, Potočari expulsions, and breakout-column mass killings
theater_description: Srebrenica town and enclave, the Potočari UN compound and nearby factories/fields, and the breakout corridor from Jaglići/Šušnjari through Konjević Polje and Nova Kasaba toward Tuzla.
geometry_method_note: Use a composite theater: enclave node around Srebrenica-Potočari plus a linear escape/capture corridor. Do not use Bosnia-wide denominators.
military_area_note: Military exposure is counted primarily among men in the breakout column, using ICTY evidence that roughly one third were 28th Division soldiers.
civilian_area_note: Civilian exposure is counted among the Potočari crowd and civilian men mixed into the breakout column; displacement reduces exposure duration to a few days.
split_reason: Distinct geography, concentrated July 1995 time window, and unusually defensible local exposure denominators relative to other genocide episodes.
quality_note: Publishable but fragile. Total deaths and theater are well documented, yet the soldier/civilian death split rests mainly on Bosnian Book of the Dead status coding that HRDAG says likely over-reports soldiers and under-reports civilians.

theater_description: Village destruction, mass killing, and forced flight concentrated in northern Rakhine after the 25 August 2017 attacks.
geometry_method_note: Use township/village-cluster polygons rather than a Myanmar-wide denominator.
military_area_note: Military victim series linked to the clearance theater appear negligible or too poorly specified for DR; episode may be censored toward zero or non-estimable.
civilian_area_note: Civilian population exposure changed rapidly because of mass flight into Bangladesh.
split_reason: Township-level exposure is possible for civilians, but not yet for a meaningful military denominator.
quality_note: Screened only. Likely a censored or non-estimable DR case because civilian-targeted killing overwhelms any documented military victim denominator.
